description: Reference to the global `String` constructor.
Tests if a value is a `string`.
examples:
import { String } from "effect"
import * as assert from "node:assert"

assert.deepStrictEqual(String.isString("a"), true)
assert.deepStrictEqual(String.isString(1), false)

description: `Order` instance for comparing strings using lexicographic ordering.
examples:
import { String } from "effect"

console.log(String.Order("apple", "banana")) // -1
console.log(String.Order("banana", "apple")) // 1
console.log(String.Order("apple", "apple")) // 0

description: An `Equivalence` instance for strings using strict equality (`===`).
examples:
import { String } from "effect"

console.log(String.Equivalence("hello", "hello")) // true
console.log(String.Equivalence("hello", "world")) // false

description: The empty string `""`.
examples:
import { String } from "effect"

console.log(String.empty) // ""
console.log(String.isEmpty(String.empty)) // true

description: Concatenates two strings at the type level.
Concatenates two strings at runtime.
Converts a string to uppercase.
examples:
import { pipe, String } from "effect"
import * as assert from "node:assert"

assert.deepStrictEqual(pipe("a", String.toUpperCase), "A")
assert.deepStrictEqual(String.toUpperCase("hello"), "HELLO")

description: Converts a string to lowercase.
examples:
import { pipe, String } from "effect"
import * as assert from "node:assert"

assert.deepStrictEqual(pipe("A", String.toLowerCase), "a")
assert.deepStrictEqual(String.toLowerCase("HELLO"), "hello")

description: Capitalizes the first character of a string.
examples:
import { pipe, String } from "effect"
import * as assert from "node:assert"

assert.deepStrictEqual(pipe("abc", String.capitalize), "Abc")
assert.deepStrictEqual(String.capitalize("hello"), "Hello")

description: Uncapitalizes the first character of a string.
examples:
import { pipe, String } from "effect"
import * as assert from "node:assert"

assert.deepStrictEqual(pipe("ABC", String.uncapitalize), "aBC")
assert.deepStrictEqual(String.uncapitalize("Hello"), "hello")

description: Replaces matches in a string using `String.prototype.replace`. String search values and non-global regular expressions replace the first match; global regular expressions replace every match.
examples:
import { pipe, String } from "effect"
import * as assert from "node:assert"

assert.deepStrictEqual(pipe("abc", String.replace("b", "d")), "adc")
assert.deepStrictEqual(
  pipe("hello world", String.replace("world", "Effect")),
  "hello Effect"
)

description: Type-level representation of trimming whitespace from both ends of a string.
examples:
import type { String } from "effect"

type Result = String.Trim<"  hello  "> // "hello"

description: Removes whitespace from both ends of a string.
examples:
import { String } from "effect"
import * as assert from "node:assert"

assert.deepStrictEqual(String.trim(" a "), "a")
assert.deepStrictEqual(String.trim("  hello world  "), "hello world")

description: Type-level representation of trimming whitespace from the start of a string.
examples:
import type { String } from "effect"

type Result = String.TrimStart<"  hello"> // "hello"

description: Removes whitespace from the start of a string.
examples:
import { String } from "effect"
import * as assert from "node:assert"

assert.deepStrictEqual(String.trimStart(" a "), "a ")
assert.deepStrictEqual(String.trimStart("  hello world"), "hello world")

description: Type-level representation of trimming whitespace from the end of a string.
examples:
import type { String } from "effect"

type Result = String.TrimEnd<"hello  "> // "hello"

description: Removes whitespace from the end of a string.
examples:
import { String } from "effect"
import * as assert from "node:assert"

assert.deepStrictEqual(String.trimEnd(" a "), " a")
assert.deepStrictEqual(String.trimEnd("hello world  "), "hello world")

description: Extracts a section of a string and returns it as a new string.
examples:
import { pipe, String } from "effect"
import * as assert from "node:assert"

assert.deepStrictEqual(pipe("abcd", String.slice(1, 3)), "bc")
assert.deepStrictEqual(pipe("hello world", String.slice(0, 5)), "hello")

description: Test whether a `string` is empty.
examples:
import { String } from "effect"
import * as assert from "node:assert"

assert.deepStrictEqual(String.isEmpty(""), true)
assert.deepStrictEqual(String.isEmpty("a"), false)

description: Test whether a `string` is non empty.
examples:
import { String } from "effect"
import * as assert from "node:assert"

assert.deepStrictEqual(String.isNonEmpty(""), false)
assert.deepStrictEqual(String.isNonEmpty("a"), true)

description: Returns the JavaScript string length, measured in UTF-16 code units.
examples:
import { String } from "effect"
import * as assert from "node:assert"

assert.deepStrictEqual(String.length("abc"), 3)

description: Splits a string into an array of substrings using a separator.
examples:
import { pipe, String } from "effect"
import * as assert from "node:assert"

assert.deepStrictEqual(pipe("abc", String.split("")), ["a", "b", "c"])
assert.deepStrictEqual(pipe("", String.split("")), [""])
assert.deepStrictEqual(String.split("hello,world", ","), ["hello", "world"])

description: Returns `true` if `searchString` appears as a substring of `self`, at one or more positions that are greater than or equal to `position`; otherwise, returns `false`.
examples:
import { pipe, String } from "effect"
import * as assert from "node:assert"

assert.deepStrictEqual(pipe("hello world", String.includes("world")), true)
assert.deepStrictEqual(pipe("hello world", String.includes("foo")), false)

description: Returns `true` if the string starts with the specified search string. The `@beep/utils` wrapper supplies a type-narrowing variant that narrows to a string with the requested prefix.
examples:
import { pipe, String } from "effect"
import * as assert from "node:assert"

assert.deepStrictEqual(pipe("hello world", String.startsWith("hello")), true)
assert.deepStrictEqual(pipe("hello world", String.startsWith("world")), false)

description: Returns `true` if the string ends with the specified search string. The `@beep/utils` wrapper supplies a type-narrowing variant that narrows to a string with the requested suffix.
examples:
import { pipe, String } from "effect"
import * as assert from "node:assert"

assert.deepStrictEqual(pipe("hello world", String.endsWith("world")), true)
assert.deepStrictEqual(pipe("hello world", String.endsWith("hello")), false)

description: Returns the character code at the specified index, or `None` if the index is out of bounds.
examples:
import { String } from "effect"

String.charCodeAt("abc", 1) // Option.some(98)
String.charCodeAt("abc", 4) // Option.none()

description: Extracts characters from a string between two specified indices.
examples:
import { pipe, String } from "effect"

pipe("abcd", String.substring(1)) // "bcd"
pipe("abcd", String.substring(1, 3)) // "bc"

description: Returns the character at the specified index, or `None` if the index is out of bounds.
examples:
import { pipe, String } from "effect"

pipe("abc", String.at(1)) // Option.some("b")
pipe("abc", String.at(4)) // Option.none()

description: Returns the character at the specified index, or `None` if the index is out of bounds.
examples:
import { pipe, String } from "effect"

pipe("abc", String.charAt(1)) // Option.some("b")
pipe("abc", String.charAt(4)) // Option.none()

description: Returns the Unicode code point at the specified index, or `None` if the index is out of bounds.
examples:
import { pipe, String } from "effect"

pipe("abc", String.codePointAt(1)) // Option.some(98)
pipe("abc", String.codePointAt(10)) // Option.none()

description: Returns the index of the first occurrence of a substring, or `None` if not found.
examples:
import { pipe, String } from "effect"

pipe("abbbc", String.indexOf("b")) // Option.some(1)
pipe("abbbc", String.indexOf("z")) // Option.none()

description: Returns the index of the last occurrence of a substring, or `None` if not found.
examples:
import { pipe, String } from "effect"

pipe("abbbc", String.lastIndexOf("b")) // Option.some(3)
pipe("abbbc", String.lastIndexOf("d")) // Option.none()

description: Compares two strings using locale-aware collation, with optional locales and collator options, and returns the result as an `Ordering` (`-1`, `0`, or `1`).
examples:
import { pipe, String } from "effect"
import * as assert from "node:assert"

assert.deepStrictEqual(pipe("a", String.localeCompare("b")), -1)
assert.deepStrictEqual(pipe("b", String.localeCompare("a")), 1)
assert.deepStrictEqual(pipe("a", String.localeCompare("a")), 0)

description: Matches a string against a pattern and returns `Option.some` with the match array, or `Option.none` when the pattern does not match.
Returns an iterator over all regular expression matches in the string using native `String.prototype.matchAll` semantics.
Normalizes a string according to the specified Unicode normalization form.
examples:
import { pipe, String } from "effect"
import * as assert from "node:assert"

const str = "\u1E9B\u0323"
assert.deepStrictEqual(pipe(str, String.normalize()), "\u1E9B\u0323")
assert.deepStrictEqual(pipe(str, String.normalize("NFC")), "\u1E9B\u0323")
assert.deepStrictEqual(pipe(str, String.normalize("NFD")), "\u017F\u0323\u0307")
assert.deepStrictEqual(pipe(str, String.normalize("NFKC")), "\u1E69")
assert.deepStrictEqual(
  pipe(str, String.normalize("NFKD")),
  "\u0073\u0323\u0307"
)

description: Pads the string from the end with a given fill string to a specified length.
examples:
import { pipe, String } from "effect"
import * as assert from "node:assert"

assert.deepStrictEqual(pipe("a", String.padEnd(5)), "a    ")
assert.deepStrictEqual(pipe("a", String.padEnd(5, "_")), "a____")

description: Pads the string from the start with a given fill string to a specified length.
examples:
import { pipe, String } from "effect"
import * as assert from "node:assert"

assert.deepStrictEqual(pipe("a", String.padStart(5)), "    a")
assert.deepStrictEqual(pipe("a", String.padStart(5, "_")), "____a")

description: Repeats the string the specified number of times. The `@beep/utils` wrapper supplies a variant with a type-level `StringRepeat` return.
examples:
import { pipe, String } from "effect"
import * as assert from "node:assert"

assert.deepStrictEqual(pipe("a", String.repeat(5)), "aaaaa")
assert.deepStrictEqual(pipe("hello", String.repeat(3)), "hellohellohello")

description: Replaces all occurrences of a substring or pattern in a string.
examples:
import { pipe, String } from "effect"
import * as assert from "node:assert"

assert.deepStrictEqual(pipe("ababb", String.replaceAll("b", "c")), "acacc")
assert.deepStrictEqual(pipe("ababb", String.replaceAll(/ba/g, "cc")), "accbb")

description: Returns the index of the first match for a string or regular expression, or `Option.none` when no match is found.
examples:
import { String } from "effect"

String.search("ababb", "b") // Option.some(1)
String.search("ababb", /abb/) // Option.some(2)
String.search("ababb", "d") // Option.none()

description: Converts the string to lowercase according to the specified locale.
examples:
import { pipe, String } from "effect"
import * as assert from "node:assert"

const str = "\u0130"
assert.deepStrictEqual(pipe(str, String.toLocaleLowerCase("tr")), "i")

description: Converts the string to uppercase according to the specified locale.
examples:
import { pipe, String } from "effect"
import * as assert from "node:assert"

const str = "i\u0307"
assert.deepStrictEqual(pipe(str, String.toLocaleUpperCase("lt-LT")), "I")

description: Keep the specified number of characters from the start of a string. If `n` is larger than the available number of characters, the string will be returned whole. If `n` is not a positive number, an empty string will be returned. If `n` is a float, it will be rounded down to the nearest integer.
examples:
import { String } from "effect"
import * as assert from "node:assert"

assert.deepStrictEqual(String.takeLeft("Hello World", 5), "Hello")

description: Keep the specified number of characters from the end of a string. If `n` is larger than the available number of characters, the string will be returned whole. If `n` is not a positive number, an empty string will be returned. If `n` is a float, it will be rounded down to the nearest integer.
examples:
import { String } from "effect"
import * as assert from "node:assert"

assert.deepStrictEqual(String.takeRight("Hello World", 5), "World")

description: Returns an `IterableIterator` which yields each line contained within the string, trimming off the trailing newline character.
Returns an `IterableIterator` which yields each line contained within the string as well as the trailing newline character.
examples:
import { String } from "effect"

const lines = String.linesWithSeparators("hello
world
")
console.log(Array.from(lines)) // ["hello
", "world
"]

description: For every line in this string, strip a leading prefix consisting of blanks or control characters followed by the character specified by `marginChar` from the line.
examples:
import { String } from "effect"

const text = "  |hello\n  |world"
const result = String.stripMarginWith(text, "|")
console.log(result) // "hello\nworld"

description: For every line in this string, strip a leading prefix consisting of blanks or control characters followed by the `"|"` character from the line.
examples:
import { String } from "effect"

const text = "  |hello\n  |world"
const result = String.stripMargin(text)
console.log(result) // "hello\nworld"

description: Converts a snake_case string to camelCase. The `@beep/utils` wrapper supplies a variant with a type-level `CamelCase` return.
examples:
import { String } from "effect"

console.log(String.snakeToCamel("hello_world")) // "helloWorld"
console.log(String.snakeToCamel("foo_bar_baz")) // "fooBarBaz"

description: Converts a snake_case string to PascalCase. The `@beep/utils` wrapper supplies a variant with a type-level `PascalCase` return.
examples:
import { String } from "effect"

console.log(String.snakeToPascal("hello_world")) // "HelloWorld"
console.log(String.snakeToPascal("foo_bar_baz")) // "FooBarBaz"

description: Converts a snake_case string to kebab-case. The `@beep/utils` wrapper supplies a variant with a type-level `KebabCase` return.
examples:
import { String } from "effect"

console.log(String.snakeToKebab("hello_world")) // "hello-world"
console.log(String.snakeToKebab("foo_bar_baz")) // "foo-bar-baz"

description: Converts a camelCase string to snake_case. The `@beep/utils` wrapper supplies a variant with a type-level `SnakeCase` return.
examples:
import { String } from "effect"

console.log(String.camelToSnake("helloWorld")) // "hello_world"
console.log(String.camelToSnake("fooBarBaz")) // "foo_bar_baz"

description: Converts a PascalCase string to snake_case. The `@beep/utils` wrapper supplies a variant with type-level `PascalCase`/`SnakeCase` types.
examples:
import { String } from "effect"

console.log(String.pascalToSnake("HelloWorld")) // "hello_world"
console.log(String.pascalToSnake("FooBarBaz")) // "foo_bar_baz"

description: Converts a kebab-case string to snake_case. The `@beep/utils` wrapper supplies a variant with a type-level `SnakeCase` return.
examples:
import { String } from "effect"

console.log(String.kebabToSnake("hello-world")) // "hello_world"
console.log(String.kebabToSnake("foo-bar-baz")) // "foo_bar_baz"

description: Normalizes a string by splitting it into word parts, transforming each part, and joining the parts with a configurable delimiter.
Converts a string to PascalCase. The `@beep/utils` wrapper supplies a variant with a type-level `PascalCase` return.
Converts a string to camelCase. The `@beep/utils` wrapper supplies a variant with a type-level `CamelCase` return.
Converts a string to CONSTANT_CASE (uppercase with underscores).
Converts a string to kebab-case (lowercase with hyphens). The `@beep/utils` wrapper supplies a variant with a type-level `KebabCase` return.
Converts a string to snake_case (lowercase with underscores). The `@beep/utils` wrapper supplies a variant with a type-level `SnakeCase` return.
A `Reducer` for concatenating `string`s.
Prepends `prefix` to a string, preserving template-literal types. Supports both data-first and data-last calling conventions. (@beep/utils wrapper-only addition.)

Prepends `prefix` to a string and returns a thunk of the result. Useful for deferred evaluation when building lazy configuration values. Supports both data-first and data-last calling conventions. (@beep/utils wrapper-only addition.)
Appends `postfix` to a string, preserving template-literal types. Supports both data-first and data-last calling conventions. (@beep/utils wrapper-only addition.)

Appends `postfix` to a string and returns a thunk of the result. Useful for deferred evaluation when building lazy configuration values. Supports both data-first and data-last calling conventions. (@beep/utils wrapper-only addition.)

Maps a non-empty string array by prepending each element with `prefix`. Preserves `NonEmptyReadonlyArray` in the return type. Supports both data-first and data-last calling conventions. (@beep/utils wrapper-only addition.)

Maps a non-empty string array by appending each element with `postfix`. Preserves `NonEmptyReadonlyArray` in the return type. Supports both data-first and data-last calling conventions. (@beep/utils wrapper-only addition.)
Converts a string to `SCREAMING_SNAKE_CASE` with a type-level `ScreamingSnakeCase` return. (@beep/utils wrapper-only addition.)

Type-narrowing predicate that checks whether a string contains `searchString`. Narrows the type to a string that contains the searched substring on success. Supports both data-first and data-last calling conventions. (@beep/utils wrapper-only addition.)

Replaces the first occurrence of `searchValue` using a callback replacer. Supports both data-first and data-last calling conventions. Use this helper for native `replace` callback sites that cannot be expressed with `Str.replace(searchValue, replacement)`. (@beep/utils wrapper-only addition.)

Replaces every occurrence of `searchValue` using a callback replacer. Supports both data-first and data-last calling conventions. Use this helper for native `replaceAll` callback sites that cannot be expressed with `Str.replaceAll(searchValue, replacement)`. (@beep/utils wrapper-only addition.)
Returns a thunk that lazily trims whitespace from both ends of a string. (@beep/utils wrapper-only addition.)
Convert a numeric literal into its string-literal representation. (@beep/utils wrapper-only addition.)
Converts arbitrary text into a lowercase kebab-case slug. (@beep/utils wrapper-only addition.)

Trim text and truncate it to the requested visible character count. When the trimmed text is longer than `maxLength`, the result keeps the first `maxLength` characters and appends `...`. (@beep/utils wrapper-only addition.)
